hook.text: Send spam from your server and it lands on a blocklist. Now every email you send gets rejected.

buildup.text: DNS-based blocklists (DNSBLs) track IPs known to send spam. Receivers query them in real time.

discovery.text: Spamhaus, Barracuda, and SpamCop maintain major lists. One listing can block your mail worldwide.

twist.text: Shared hosting means shared IPs. One spammy neighbor can get your innocent server blocklisted too.

climax.text: Reputation is rebuilt slowly: clean sending, low bounce rates, and proper authentication. Days to weeks.

punchline.text: IP reputation is email currency. Lose it and delivery stops.

quiz.question: What is a DNSBL?
options: A DNS-based list of IPs known to send spam
A tool for compressing email attachments
